He said, "ego eimi the Bread of that came down out of Heaven." |
He said, "I am the Bread of that came down out of Heaven." |
| 6:48 |
He said, "ego eimi the Bread of that came down out of Heaven." |
He said, "I am the Bread of that came down out of Heaven." |
| 6:51 |
"ego eimi the Living Bread that came down out of Heaven." |
"I am the Living Bread that came down out of Heaven." |
| 7:34 |
"Where eimi ego you cannot come." |
"Where I am you cannot come." |
| 8:12 |
"ego eimi the Liight of the World." |
"I am the Light of the World." |
| 8:12 |
"ego eimi one who testifies." |
"I am one who testifies." |
| 8:23 |
"ego out of the above eimi." |
"I am out of the above." |
| 8:18 |
"ego eimi he who testifies." |
"I am he who testifies." |
| 8:23 |
"ego not eimi out of the world." |
"I am not out of the world." |
| 8:24 |
"If you believe not that ego eimi, you will die in your sins." |
"If you do not believe that I am [the Light from above] you will die in your sins." See. 8:25 and 8;12,23. |
| 8:28 |
"When you have lifted up the Son of Man then you shall know that ego eimi." |
"When you have lifted up the Son of Man then you shall know that I am [the Light of the World]." |
| 8:58 |
"Before Abraham was ego eimi." |
"I am before Abraham." "Before Abraham was I am." See John 1:30. |
| 9:9 |
Some said, "It is he." Others said he is like him. But he said, "ego eimi." |
Some said, "It is he." Others said he is like him. But he [the blind man] said, "I am." |
| 10:7 |
"ego eimi the door of the sheep." |
I am the door of the sheep." |
| 10:9 |
"ego eimi the door." |
I am the door." |
| 10:11,14 |
"ego eimi the Good Shepherd." |
I am the Good Shepherd." |
| 11:25 |
"ego eimi the Resurrection and the Life." |
I am the Resurrection and the Life." |
| 12:26 |
"Where eimi ego there my servant also shall be." |
Where I am there my servant also shall be." |
| 13:19 |
"From now on I am telling you before it comes to pass, so that when it does occur, you may believe that ego eimi." |
From now on I am telling you before it comes to pass, so that when it does occur, you may believe that I am." |
| 14:3 |
"Where "ego eimi you may be also" |
Where "I am you may be also" |
| 14:6 |
"Jesus said, "ego eimi the Way and the Truth and the Life." |
Jesus said, "I am the Way and the Truth and the Life." |
| 15:5 |
""ego eimi the Way and the Vine and you are the branches." |
"I am the Vine and you are the branches." |
| 17:14 |
"ego not eimi out of the world." |
"I am not out of the world." |
| 17:16 |
"ego out of the world not eimi." |
"I am not out of the world." |
| 18:5 |
"Whom do you seek?" They answered Him, "Jesus the Nazarene." Jesus said to them, "ego eimi." |

| 18:6 |
When he said to them, "ego out eimi" the fell backward to the ground. |
When he said to them, "I am they fell backward to the ground |
| 18:8 |
Therefore He again asked them, "Whom do you seek?" And they said, "Jesus the Nazarene." Jesus answered, "I told you that ego eimi, so if you seek Me, let these go their way." |

"You say that eimi ego a King." |
"You say that I am a King." |
| 18:37 |
"You say that eimi ego a King." |
"You say that I am a King." |

title: The Trinity Delusion: Quotable Quotes
content:
Quotable Quotes
"And Thomas answered and said to him, 'My Lord and my God.'"
Athanasius |
God became man so that men might become gods. |
Gregory of Nyssa
|
If in this city you ask a shopkeeper for change, he will argue with you about whether the Son is begotten or unbegotten. If you inquire about the quality of the bread, the baker will answer, "The Father is greater, the Son is less." And if you ask the bath attendant to draw your bath, he will tell you that the Son was created ex nihilo. |
Augustine |
Who understands the omnipotent Trinity? Yet who among us does not speak of it, if indeed it is the Trinity he speaks of? |
Augustine |
[John 17:3]: The proper order of the words is, "That they may know Thee and Jesus Christ, whom Thou hast sent, as the only true God." Consequently, therefore, the Holy Spirit is also understood, because He is the Spirit of the Father and Son, as the substantial and consubstantial love of both. For the Father and Son are not two Gods, nor are the Father and Son and Holy Spirit three Gods; but the Trinity itself is the one only true God. |
Thomas Aquinas |
The truth that God is three and one is altogether a matter of faith; and in no way can it be demonstratively proved. |
John Calvin |
The ancients made a wrong use of [John 10:30] to prove that Christ is of the same essence with the Father. For Christ does not argue about the unity of substance, but about the agreement that he has with the Father so that whatever is done by Christ will be confirmed by the power of his Father. (Commentary on John). |
John Calvin |
If [Servetus] comes [to Geneva], I shall never let him go out alive if my authority has weight. (Commentary on John). (Calvin to Farel, Feb. 13, 1546). |
John Calvin |
Honour, glory, and riches shall be the reward of your pains; but above all, do not fail to rid the country of those scoundrels, who stir up the people to revolt against us. Such monsters should be exterminated, as I have exterminated Michael Servetus the Spaniard. (Calvin to the Marquis Paet, high chamberlain to the King of Navarre, 1561). |
John Calvin |
Let Baudouin abuse me as long as he will, provided that, by the judgment of Melanchthon, posterity owes me a debt of gratitude for having purged the Church of so pernicious a monster. |
Michael Servetus |
To kill a man is not to defend a doctrine, but to kill a man |
Michael Servetus |
It is an invention of the devil, an infernal falsity for the destruction of all Christianity.
|
Michael Servetus |
I do not separate Christ from God more than a voice from the speaker or a beam from the sun. Christ is the voice of the speaker. He and the Father are the same thing, as the beam and the light, are the same light. |
Michael Servetus |
[Last words before being burned at the stake]: "Jesu, thou Son of the eternal God, have compassion upon me!"
|
Isaac Newton |
If the ancient churches in debating and deciding the greatest mysteries of religion, knew nothing of these two texts [1 Timothy 3:16 and 1 John 5:7], I understand not, why we should be so fond of them now the debates are over. |
Contemporary Scholars and Theologians
J.A.T. Robinson |
[Hebrews 1:8]: 'God is thy throne' or 'Thy throne is God.' Either makes good sense." (Word Pictures in the New Testament |
B.F. Westcott |
[Hebrews 1:8]: Thus on the whole it seems BEST to adopt in the first clause the rendering: `God is thy throne' (or, `Thy throne is God'), that is, `Thy kingdom is founded upon God, the immovable Rock.'" (The Epistle to the Hebrews, 1889) |
Contemporary Trinitarian Apologists
Sometimes men such as these just don't realize what they are confessing.
James R. White |
[The Trinity] is the topic we don't want to talk about: no one dares question the Trinity for fear of being branded a "heretic," yet we have all sorts of questions about it, and we aren't sure who we can ask. (The Forgotten Trinity, p. 14, emphasis mine).
|
James R. White |
The one what is the one Being or essence of God; the three who's are the Father, Son, and Spirit. We dare not mix up the what's and who's regarding the Trinity. (The Forgotten Trinity, p. 27).
|
Robert Bowman |
Thus, God refers to himself as "I," and is addressed by humans as "you," in the singular. This is no embarassment to the Trinitarian belief, but it fits perfectly, since Trinitarians believe that the "three persons" are one divine being. (Why You Should Believe in the Trinity, p. 14, emphasis mine).
|
Robert Bowman |
Christians who believe in the Trinity and have studied the doctrine freely admit that they cannot fully comphrend it but they deny that it is confusing.(Why You Should Believe in the Trinity, p. 17).
|
Robert Bowman |
But in 381 the emperor Theodosius, who held to the Trinity, declared trinitarian Christianity the official religion of the empire and convened the Council of Constantinople, where an even more explicit trinitarian creed was adopted. (Why You Should Believe in the Trinity, p. 40).
|
Robert Bowman |
[Concerning Hebrews 2:7, Bowman insists that the inspired Hebrews writer's quotation and understanding of Psalm 8:5 is incorrect and does not refer to angels]: First, it is questionable that in its original context elohim in Psalm 8:5 should be understood to refer to angels.... Psalm 8:5 is meant to be understood as saying that man is a little lower than God, not angels. (Why You Should Believe in the Trinity, p. 52).
|
Last Updated: February 19, 2011
|
|
|

title: The Trinity Delusion: Christ - God's Anointed One
content:
Christ: God's Anointed One
The English words "Christ" and "Messiah" are not translated words; they are anglicized words. The word "Christ" is an anglicized form of the Greek word "Christos." Likewise, the English word "Messiah" is an anglicized form of the Hebrew word "Mashiach." All of these words mean exactly the same thing: Anointed One. Unfortunately, translators sometimes anglicized "Christos" in our Bibles and sometimes translated it. Whenever this Greek word refers to Jesus, they anglicized it but whenever it referred to someone else they translated it and thereby obscured the connection between the two for English speaking readers. For example, King Cyrus of Persia is called God's Mashiach (Isaiah 45:1) which in Greek is God's Christos and in English God's Messiah or God's Christ or God's Anointed One (all the terms mean exactly the same thing). However, this verse is usually translated as "God's Anointed" since Trinitarian translators, and Trinitarians themselves, do not like the sound of calling anyone else but Jesus "God's Christ." But the Scriptures do.
Saul, David, Solomon: God's Christ/Messiah/Anointed One
In the Bible, the Kings of Israel were God's Christ, His Anointed One. Saul, David, and Solomon were God's Messiah. The word in question in all of the following passages is the Hebrew word "Mashiach" (Messiah) and in the Greek translation, the word "Christos" (Christ).
God's Anointed One
| Verse | Mashiach = Messiah = Christos = Christ = Anointed One |
|
1 Samuel 2:10
|
And He will give strength to His King, and will exalt the horn of His Messiah/Christ.
|
1 Samuel 12:3,5 King Saul
See 1 Samuel 8:1ff. where Israel rejects God as their King and asked for a human King.
|
Samuel said to all Israel, "Behold, I have listened to your voice in all that you said to me and I have appointed a King over you. Now, here is the King walking before you... Here I am; testify against me before the LORD and His Messiah/Christ... The LORD is a witness against you, and His Messiah/Christ is witness this day that you have found nothing in my hand....When you saw that Nahash the King of the sons of Ammon came against you, you said to me, "No, but a King shall reign over us," although the LORD your God was your King. Now therefore, here is the King whom you have chosen, whom you have asked for, and behold, the LORD has set a King over you.
|
1 Samuel 24:6,10 King Saul
|
Far be it from me because of the Lord that I should do this thing to my Lord [Saul], the LORD's Messiah/Christ, to stretch out my hand against him, since he is Yahweh's Messiah/Christ....I will not stretch out my hand against my Lord [Saul], for he is the LORD's Messiah/Christ.
|
1 Samuel 26:9,11,16,23 King Saul
|
David said to Abishai, �Do not destroy him, for who can stretch out his hand against the LORD�s Messiah/Christ and be without guilt?.... The LORD forbid that I should stretch out my hand against the LORD�s Messiah/Christ.... As the LORD lives, all of you must surely die, because you did not guard your Lord [Saul], the LORD�s Messiah/Christ.... I refused to stretch out my hand against the LORD�s Messiah/Christ.... Then Saul said to David, "Blessed are you, my son David."
|
2 Samuel 1:14,16 King Saul
|
David said to him, "How is it you were not afraid to stretch out your hand to destroy the LORD�s Messiah/Christ?... Your blood is on your head, for your mouth has testified against you, saying, �I have killed the LORD�s Messiah/Christ."
|
2 Samuel 19:21 King David
|
Should not Shimei be put to death for this, because he cursed the LORD�s Messiah/Christ?
|
2 Samuel 22:51-23:1 King David
|
[The LORD] is a tower of deliverance to His King, and shows lovingkindness to His Messiah/Christ, to David and his descendants forever. Now these are the last words of David.
David the son of Jesse declares, the man who was raised on High declares, the Messiah/Christ of the God of Jacob, and the sweet psalmist of Israel...
|
2 Chronicles 6:42 King David
|
O LORD God, do not turn away the face of Your Messiah/Christ; remember Your lovingkindness to Your servant David.�
|
Also see 1 Chronicles 16:22; Psalm 2:2 (Acts 4:26); 18:50; 20:6; 28:8; 84:9; 89:38,51; 105:15; 132:10,17; Isaiah 45:1.
Although it is not explicitly stated in Scripture, it is reasonably certain that Solomon was also God's Christ. This is implied by the fact that he sat on the throne of Yahweh (1 Chronicles 29:23) over the Kingdom of God (1 Chronicles 28:5). God's Anointed was conditionally expected to obey the Law. Beginning with Solomon's worship of foreign gods, the Kingdom is fallen. See Acts 1:6-7.
David was God's Christ, the man who sat on the throne of Yahweh. God promised David that his seed would sit upon this throne. Jesus was the fulfillment of God's promise to David. Like David, he was God's Christ, His Anointed One. It is important to see that Jesus was not just the Christ; he was God's Christ, His Anointed. The man Jesus is someone who God anointed to carry out His purposes. The following illustrates how various passages would appear in the Scriptures depending how the Greek word is rendered: (1) Christ, (2) Messiah, (3) Anointed One. As already explained, the words "Christ" and "Messiah" are not technically translations but anglicizations. A true translation uses the English term "Anointed One."
God's "Christ"
And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ. And inspired by the Spirit he came into the temple and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the law, he took him up in his arms and blessed God and said, "Lord, now let your servant depart in peace, according to your word for mine eyes have seen Your salvation. (Luke 2:26-30).
"The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty those who are oppressed, to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord." And he closed the book, and gave it back to the attendant, and sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him. And he began to say to them, "Today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing." (Luke 4:18-20).
And He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" And Peter answered and said, "The Christ of God (Or 'God's Christ')." (Luke 9:29).
And the people stood by, looking on. And even the rulers were sneering at Him, saying, "He saved others; let Him save Himself if this is God's Christ, His Chosen One. (Luke 23:35).
Therefore let all the house of Israel know for certain that God has made Him, this Jesus whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ. (Acts 2:36).
But the things which God announced beforehand by the mouth of all the prophets, that His Christ would suffer, He has thus fulfilled. Therefore repent and return, so that your sins may be wiped away, in order that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord; and that He may send Jesus, the Christ appointed for you. (Acts 3:18-20).
The kings of the earth took their stand, And the rulers were gathered together Against YAHWEH and against His Messiah. For truly against Your holy Servant Jesus, whom You anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel, were gathered together to do whatever Your hand and Your purpose determined before to be done. (Acts 4:26).
God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him. (Acts 10:38).
You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness. Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of gladness more than Your companions. (Hebrews 1:9)
Then the seventh angel sounded; and there were loud voices in heaven, saying, "The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Christ and He will reign forever and ever." (Revelation 11:15.
Then I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying, "Now the salvation, and the power, and the kingdom of our God and the authority of His Christ have come, for the accuser of our brethren has been thrown down, he who accuses them before our God day and night. (Revelation 12:10).
God's "Messiah"
And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord's Messiah. And inspired by the Spirit he came into the temple and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the law, he took him up in his arms and blessed God and said, "Lord, now let your servant depart in peace, according to your word for mine eyes have seen Your salvation. (Luke 2:26-30).
And He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" And Peter answered and said, "The Messiah of God (Or 'God's Messiah')." (Luke 9:29).
"The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty those who are oppressed, to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord." And he closed the book, and gave it back to the attendant, and sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him. And he began to say to them, "Today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing." (Luke 4:18-20).
And the people stood by, looking on. And even the rulers were sneering at Him, saying, "He saved others; let Him save Himself if this is God's Messiah, His Chosen One. (Luke 23:35).
Therefore let all the house of Israel know for certain that God has made Him, this Jesus whom you crucified, both Lord and Messiah. (Acts 2:36).
But the things which God announced beforehand by the mouth of all the prophets, that His Messiah would suffer, He has thus fulfilled. Therefore repent and return, so that your sins may be wiped away, in order that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord; and that He may send Jesus, the Messiah appointed for you. (Acts 3:18-20).
The kings of the earth took their stand, And the rulers were gathered together Against YAHWEH and against His Messiah. For truly against Your holy Servant Jesus, whom You anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel, were gathered together to do whatever Your hand and Your purpose determined before to be done. (Acts 4:26).
God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him. (Acts 10:38).
You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness. Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of gladness more than Your companions. (Hebrews 1:9)
Then the seventh angel sounded; and there were loud voices in heaven, saying, "The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Messiah and He will reign forever and ever." (Revelation 11:15.
Then I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying, "Now the salvation, and the power, and the kingdom of our God and the authority of His Messiah have come, for the accuser of our brethren has been thrown down, he who accuses them before our God day and night. (Revelation 12:10).
God's "Anointed One"
And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord's Anointed One. And inspired by the Spirit he came into the temple and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him according to the custom of the law, he took him up in his arms and blessed God and said, "Lord, now let your servant depart in peace, according to your word for mine eyes have seen Your salvation. (Luke 2:26-30).
"The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty those who are oppressed, to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord." And he closed the book, and gave it back to the attendant, and sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him. And he began to say to them, "Today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing." (Luke 4:18-20).
And He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" And Peter answered and said, "The Anointed One of God (Or 'God's Christ')." (Luke 9:29).
And the people stood by, looking on. And even the rulers were sneering at Him, saying, "He saved others; let Him save Himself if this is God's Anointed One, His Chosen One. (Luke 23:35).
Therefore let all the house of Israel know for certain that God has made Him, this Jesus whom you crucified, both Lord and Anointed One. (Acts 2:36).
But what God foretold by the mouth of all the prophets, that His Anointed One should suffer, he thus fulfilled. Therefore repent and return, so that your sins may be wiped away, in order that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord; and that He may send Jesus, the Anointed One appointed for you. (Acts 3:18-20).
The kings of the earth took their stand, And the rulers were gathered together Against YAHWEH and against Anointed One. For truly against Your holy Servant Jesus, whom You anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel, were gathered together to do whatever Your hand and Your purpose determined before to be done. (Acts 4:26).
God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him. (Acts 10:38).
You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness. Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of gladness more than Your companions. (Hebrews 1:9)
Then the seventh angel sounded; and there were loud voices in heaven, saying, "The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Anointed One and He will reign forever and ever." (Revelation 11:15.
Then I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying, "Now the salvation, and the power, and the kingdom of our God and the authority of His Anointed One have come, for the accuser of our brethren has been thrown down, he who accuses them before our God day and night. (Revelation 12:10).
